meta.mission: Touch all 435 problems of the Striver A2Z / TUF+ sheet in 20 days via two-tier engagement: Tier A full solves, Tier B recognition passes. Sheet 100% touched by end of Day 18; Days 19-20 are integration.
meta._note: TEMPLATE VALUES. Your real settings live in data/config.json (created on first run, gitignored); the server re-bases these 20 days onto your start date in memory. Run `npm run setup` to change them.

blocks.name: Overflow + warm-up
blocks.content: overflow queue, post-contest upsolve, one warm-up re-solve

blocks.name: New topic
blocks.content: lecture at 2x + canonical Tier A solves

blocks.name: Dinner
blocks.content: movement already covered by morning gym

blocks.name: Speed drill
blocks.content: timed re-solves (odd days) or CF ladder (even days)

blocks.name: Review
blocks.content: pattern cards, shuffle review, day log, evidence card

schedule_template.sunday_variant: LC Weekly 08:00-09:30 IST replaces or lightens gym; anchor immediately after contest; contest problems credit Tier A quota.
schedule_template.cf_night_variant: Round 20:05-22:05 IST replaces Blocks 4-5; next morning Block 0 contains exactly one upsolve.

rules.solve_protocol: 10/10/35: 0-10 min solo (classification typed before timer starts); checkpoint gate at 10 (continue, read approach only, or ask Coach); hard ceiling at 35 (solved, or read full solution then immediate re-implement from blank); non-solo outcomes logged; non-solved problems flagged for Day 19-20 pool.
rules.debug_rule: Bug surviving 10 minutes: stop re-reading code, dry-run smallest failing input on paper or print-trace.
rules.pattern_card: Every solve and every Tier B pass ends with a 60-second card: Trigger / Pattern / Trap.
rules.overflow: Unfinished Tier A rolls to next morning Block 0. Nothing silently dropped.
rules.win_condition: Day is WON iff quota (possibly compressed) is met, regardless of hint usage.
rules.bad_day_protocol: One button: trim tomorrow Tier B by 3, move remainder to overflow, close day. Never extend past sleep guard.
rules.dp_keep_warm: Days 1-5 only: Block 5 includes one 15-minute re-implement-from-blank of an earlier DP problem (climbing stairs, frog jump, grid paths - rotate).
rules.upsolve: Morning after any contest: exactly one upsolve (first unsolved problem) - editorial, close, re-implement from blank, card.

lc_biweekly.pattern: alternate Saturdays - user toggles which are live after checking parity

codeforces.live_rules: Div 3 and Div 4 live, cap 3 rounds during Days 1-18; Div 2 only Days 19-20

supplements: Count Square Submatrices with All Ones
Capacity to Ship Packages in D Days
Flatten Binary Tree to Linked List
read_only: Minimize Max Distance to Gas Station

tierA: Sort an array of 0s 1s 2s (Dutch National Flag)
Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock I
tierB: Left Rotate Array by K Places
days.note: Clear the entire Fundamentals + Logic Building block as Tier B - 2-3 minutes each.

tierA: Count Subarrays with Sum K (prefix sum + hashmap)
Find the Repeating and Missing Number
tierB: Merge Two Sorted Arrays Without Extra Space
days.note: Crown pattern: prefix-sum + hashmap. Sum K, sum 0 and XOR K are one idea in three costumes. Sorting reads: selection/bubble/insertion/quick — merge is covered by Count Inversions, which also checks its Sorting box.

tierA: Longest Substring with At Most K Distinct Characters
Number of Substrings Containing All Three Characters
tierB: Maximum Points You Can Obtain from Cards
days.note: Mantra before every solve: expand right freely, shrink left while the invariant is broken. Learn atMost(k) - atMost(k-1) properly.

tierA: Implement lower_bound and upper_bound from scratch
First and Last Occurrence in Sorted Array
Search in Rotated Sorted Array I
Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
Single Element in a Sorted Array
tierB: Floor and Ceil in Sorted Array
Search in Rotated Sorted Array II
Square Root and Nth Root via BS
Find How Many Times Array Has Been Rotated
days.note: One mental model owns the topic: every binary search finds the first index where a monotone predicate flips false to true.

tierB: Capacity to Ship Packages in D Days
Minimum Days to Make M Bouquets
Kth Element of Two Sorted Arrays
days.note: BS-on-answer is the 1:1 interview/Codeforces crossover - today pays double. Minimize Max Distance to Gas Station is a Day 18 read-only.
Four-variant matrix: {0/1, unbounded} x {optimize, count}. Full 4-step pipeline on Subset Sum and 0/1 Knapsack only; memoization-only on the rest.

tierA: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II
Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock III
days.note: The LCS table is the mother - six Tier B problems today are arithmetic on it.

tierA: LIS in n log n (binary search)
Count Square Submatrices with All Ones
tierB: Minimum Cost to Cut the Stick
days.note: Burst Balloons insight: think about which balloon pops LAST. Maximal Rectangle deferred to Day 15 (needs histogram).

tierA: Traversal block: inorder + preorder + postorder + level order
tierB: Pre, Post, Inorder in One Traversal
Check if Two Trees are Identical
days.note: One pattern powers four solves: return information upward, update the answer globally.

tierA: Lowest Common Ancestor in Binary Tree
Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder
tierB: Requirements to Construct a Unique BT (theory)
Minimum Time to Burn Binary Tree
Count Nodes in Complete Binary Tree
Flatten Binary Tree to Linked List
Insert a Given Node in BST
Deletion of the Head of LL
Deletion of the Tail of LL
Deletion of the Kth Element of LL
Delete the Element with Value X
Insertion at the Head of LL
Insertion at the Tail of LL
Insertion at the Kth Position of LL
Insertion Before the Value X in LL
Convert Array to Doubly Linked List
Delete Kth Element of Doubly LL
Removing Given Node in Doubly LL
Insert Node Before Head in Doubly LL
Insert Node Before Tail in Doubly LL
Insert Node Before Kth Node in Doubly LL
Insert Before Given Node in Doubly LL
Print All Primes till N (Sieve of Eratosthenes)
Count Primes in Range L to R
days.note: BST is a half-day; the 24 LL CRUD items run 1-3 minutes each - a recognition marathon, not a grind. Card binary exponentiation alongside the sieve trio. Evening graph-representation re-skim.

tierB: Number of Ways to Arrive at Destination
City with Smallest Number of Neighbours
days.note: Word Ladder II is a Day 18 read-only.

days.focus: Graphs III: DSU + MST + graph sweep
tierA: Disjoint Set Union - by size + path compression [COACHED]
Minimum Operations to Make Network Connected
tierB: Most Stones Removed with Same Row or Column
days.note: Sweep day: every remaining graph checkbox gets touched by tonight.
The implementation eight are reads with a mental sketch - the monotonic-stack Tier A is where the day's blood goes.

tierA: Kth Largest Element in a Stream
days.note: Swap out any greedy already completed from the existing Tier 1-2 progress; tell Coach that morning and substitute the next-best.

tierA: Maximum XOR of Two Numbers (bit trie)
tierB: Add One to a Number Represented by LL
Delete the Middle Node in LL
Delete All Occurrences of a Key in DLL
Number of Distinct Substrings in a String
Maximum XOR with Element from Array
days.note: LL problems run 10-15 minutes each. Quant firms love Max XOR. CRUD fundamentals already cleared on Day 11.

days.focus: KMP + bit cleanup + THE GREAT SWEEP
tierA: KMP / LPS Array (understand the failure function)
tierB: XOR of Numbers in a Given Range
Reverse Every Word in a String
Minimize Max Distance to Gas Station
ALL remaining unchecked sheet boxes - 3 to 4 hour sweep
days.note: End of day: 435/435 touched. Generate the evidence card. This is the proof. The sweep includes Beginner-module stragglers (1-minute checks are legitimate) and verifies the backtracking set (N-Queens, Sudoku, Word Search, M-Coloring) - any unchecked join the sweep; N-Queens as a full solve if unchecked.

tasks: Re-implement the 5 worst-flagged problems from blank
days.note: Mock generator samples across topics weighted toward weak/flagged.

tasks: Second virtual contest, or live CF if one falls tonight
Final weak-spot patches from the log
Light evening - sharp, not fried, for July 2nd
days.note: End-of-sprint report: total solves, solo rate, average solve time curve, topic radar, the 77 to 435 arc.
